<!-- Generated by GSD docs-update -->

# Architecture

Screws Box is a single-binary Go web application for tracking hardware parts in physical organizer boxes. It serves an HTML UI with a JSON API, backed by an embedded SQLite database.

## System Overview

```
+------------------+      HTTP       +-------------------+
|                  | <-------------> |                   |
|  Browser / API   |                 |  cmd/screwsbox    |
|                  |                 |  (entrypoint)     |
+------------------+                 +--------+----------+
                                              |
                            +-----------------+-----------------+
                            |                 |                 |
                    +-------v------+  +-------v------+  +------v-------+
                    | server       |  | session      |  | store        |
                    | (chi router, |  | (Manager,    |  | (SQLite,     |
                    |  handlers,   |  |  Memory or   |  |  schema,     |
                    |  middleware)  |  |  Redis)      |  |  queries)    |
                    +-------+------+  +--------------+  +------+-------+
                            |                                  |
                    +-------v------+                   +-------v-------+
                    | oidc         |                   | model         |
                    | (Provider,   |                   | (domain types,|
                    |  PKCE flow)  |                   |  helpers)     |
                    +--------------+                   +---------------+
```

## Directory Structure

```
cmd/
  screwsbox/          CLI entrypoint, wires dependencies, runs HTTP server
    main.go           signal handling, env var parsing, OIDC seed, graceful shutdown
internal/
  model/              Domain types shared across packages (Shelf, Container, Item, Tag, etc.)
  store/              SQLite persistence layer (schema DDL, CRUD, export/import)
  session/            Session management with pluggable backends
    store.go          Store interface (Create, Get, Delete, Touch, List)
    memory.go         In-memory Store with TTL expiry and background cleanup
    redis.go          Redis-backed Store using go-redis/v9
    manager.go        Manager wraps Store, handles cookie read/write and CSRF tokens
    session.go        Session struct definition
  server/             HTTP layer
    routes.go         chi router setup, route registration, middleware stack
    handler.go        StoreService interface + all HTTP handler implementations
    middleware.go     Rate limiting (per-IP token bucket) and CSRF protection
    embed_prod.go     Embeds templates + static files into the binary (production)
    embed_dev.go      Reads templates + static from disk (dev build tag)
  oidc/               OpenID Connect integration
    provider.go       OIDC discovery, token exchange, claims extraction
    cookie.go         Encrypted state/nonce cookies for OIDC flow
    config.go         OIDC configuration helpers
assets/               Screenshots for documentation
```

## Key Design Decisions

### Single Binary with Embedded Assets

Production builds embed all HTML templates and static files into the binary via `go:embed` (`embed_prod.go`). The `dev` build tag (`embed_dev.go`) switches to reading from disk for live-reload during development. This means deployment is a single file copy with no external asset dependencies.

### StoreService Interface

Handlers depend on the `StoreService` interface defined in `handler.go`, not the concrete `store.Store`. This decouples the HTTP layer from SQLite specifics and makes handler testing possible with mock implementations.

### Pluggable Session Backends

The `session.Store` interface has two implementations:
- **MemoryStore** -- in-process map with background TTL cleanup (default)
- **RedisStore** -- Redis-backed for persistence across restarts

`session.Manager` wraps whichever Store is active, handling cookie mechanics (session ID + CSRF double-submit) and sliding-window expiry via `Touch()`.

### CGo-Free SQLite

Uses `modernc.org/sqlite` (a pure Go SQLite port) so the binary compiles with `CGO_ENABLED=0`. This enables `FROM scratch` Docker images and simplifies cross-compilation.

### OIDC with PKCE

The OIDC flow uses PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) with encrypted state/nonce cookies. The `oidc.Provider` wraps `go-oidc/v3` for discovery and token verification. OIDC configuration is stored in the database after initial seed from environment variables.

## Request Lifecycle

1. Request hits the chi router
2. Middleware stack executes in order:
   - `middleware.RealIP` -- extracts client IP from proxy headers
   - `middleware.Logger` -- request logging
   - `middleware.Recoverer` -- panic recovery
   - `middleware.RequestID` -- assigns request ID
   - `newRateLimitAPI()` -- per-IP token bucket (5 req/s, burst 10)
3. For protected routes, additional middleware:
   - `authMiddleware()` -- validates session cookie, redirects to `/login` if missing
   - `csrfProtect()` -- validates `X-CSRF-Token` header or `csrf_token` form field on state-changing requests
4. Handler executes, calling `StoreService` methods for data access
5. Response: HTML (via `html/template`) for pages, JSON for `/api/*` endpoints

Login routes have a separate stricter rate limiter (0.5 req/s, burst 5).

The `/healthz` endpoint is registered outside the middleware group to avoid log noise from Kubernetes probes.

## Data Model

```
shelf 1---* container 1---* item *---* tag
                                  (via item_tag)
```

- **shelf** -- one shelf per database, defines grid dimensions (rows x cols) and name
- **container** -- one per grid cell, identified by (col, row) pair; labels like "3B" are computed at runtime by `model.LabelFor()`, never stored
- **item** -- a part stored in a container (name, optional description)
- **tag** -- lowercase-normalized labels; many-to-many with items via `item_tag` junction table

All tables use `INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT` and track `created_at` / `updated_at` timestamps.

## Dependencies

| Package | Version | Purpose |
|---------|---------|---------|
| `github.com/go-chi/chi/v5` | v5.2.5 | HTTP router (stdlib-compatible) |
| `modernc.org/sqlite` | v1.48.2 | CGo-free SQLite driver |
| `github.com/coreos/go-oidc/v3` | v3.18.0 | OpenID Connect client |
| `github.com/redis/go-redis/v9` | v9.18.0 | Redis client for session storage |
| `golang.org/x/crypto` | v0.50.0 | bcrypt password hashing |
| `golang.org/x/oauth2` | v0.36.0 | OAuth2 client (used by OIDC) |
| `golang.org/x/time` | v0.15.0 | Rate limiter (`rate.Limiter`) |
| `github.com/stretchr/testify` | v1.11.1 | Test assertions |

## Build and Deployment

- **Binary**: `CGO_ENABLED=0 go build -ldflags="-s -w -X main.version=..." -o screws-box ./cmd/screwsbox`
- **Docker**: Multi-stage build (`golang:1.26-alpine` -> `scratch`), exposes port 8080
- **Docker Compose**: Optional Redis profile for persistent sessions
- **Version**: Injected at build time via `-X main.version`, defaults to `"dev"`
